pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 41-42 creates course with shortname='CHEM101'; task description confirms 'Chemistry 101 (CHEM101)'
setup_task.sh lines 57-58 lists both users; lines 117-130 set both as inactive (45 days); verifier.py line 25 expects these exact users
setup_task.sh lines 57-58 lists all three users; lines 133-138 set all three as active (2 days); verifier.py line 26 expects these exact users
Task description explicitly states 'The message body must contain the phrase 'catch up''; verified in export_result.sh line 57 and verifier.py line 27
setup_task.sh line 112 defines INACTIVE_TIME=$((NOW - (45 * DAY_SEC))) indicating 45 days of inactivity; line 164 confirms 'Users dlee and epatel set to INACTIVE (45 days)'

privileged_info_summary: The identify_at_risk_students task operates on a synthetic Moodle environment. The task requires identifying two target users (dlee, epatel) who are set to be inactive for 45 days, and three control users (fkim, awilson, bbrown) who are set to be active (2 days). The course is CHEM101. Messages must contain the phrase 'catch up'. A discrepancy exists: the metadata claims inactivity_days is 30, but the actual test data sets target users to be inactive for 45 days (not 30).
